minimum_commands: .venv/bin/python scripts/cognitive_loop_watcher_runner.py run --html --study-adapter --changed-path apps/api/study_anything/core/workflow.py --git-diff-summary "Metadata-only workflow boundary changed"
must_prove: all submission assets exist
all platform packs remain aligned
platform tool manifest excludes management endpoints
submission metadata declares no standalone frontend requirement
submission metadata declares no Study Anything model-key custody
commercial-readiness-v1 remains linked
adoption-telemetry-v1 remains aggregate-only

cognitive-loop-contract-bootstrap-v1 proves .cognitive-loop config, permissions, evals, and risk contracts are present, read-only by default, privacy-safe, and reject secret-like values, raw excerpts, and high-risk decisions without a Human Mastery Gate

cognitive-loop-cli-artifact-verification-v1 proves external operators can initialize contracts, verify them, and render a local static HTML DecisionCard artifact without a standalone frontend or model-key custody

cognitive-loop-run-once-evidence-verification-v1 proves external operators can produce one redacted LoopRun and DecisionCard evidence cycle without a watcher daemon, Mastra runtime, or model-key custody

cognitive-loop-project-snapshot-verification-v1 proves external operators can capture path-level project snapshot events without storing diff bodies, file contents, watcher state, or model-key custody

cognitive-loop-human-gate-verification-v1 proves external operators can record local Human Mastery Gate approvals and rejections without executing changes, storing private context, or taking model-key custody
cognitive-loop-evidence-bundle-verification-v1 proves external operators can bundle local evidence metadata with paths, sizes, and hashes without embedding artifact contents or private context

cognitive-loop-event-index-verification-v1 proves external operators can build a metadata-only local event timeline from Cognitive Loop artifacts without embedding event contents, starting a watcher daemon, or taking model-key custody

cognitive-loop-event-store-verification-v1 proves external operators can rebuild a local SQLite Event Store from validated artifacts, export metadata-only HTML/JSON, reject unsafe Agent endpoints, and keep watcher/Mastra/runtime claims out of the MVP

cognitive-loop-watcher-ingest-verification-v1 proves external operators can validate watcher config, manually ingest metadata-only ProjectEvent artifacts, reject excluded targets, and project watcher events into Event Index and Event Store without starting a daemon

cognitive-loop-artifact-doctor-verification-v1 proves external operators can detect missing pairs, duplicate hashes, invalid JSON, and stale local indexes or bundles without embedding artifact contents, starting a watcher daemon, or taking model-key custody

cognitive-loop-repair-plan-verification-v1 proves external operators can map artifact doctor issues to manual-only repair actions without executing file changes, embedding artifact contents, starting a watcher daemon, or taking model-key custody

cognitive-loop-artifact-index-verification-v1 proves external operators can open a static local artifact index with relative links, hashes, and metadata without embedding artifact contents, requiring a standalone frontend, starting a watcher daemon, or taking model-key custody

cognitive-loop-adoption-cookbook-verification-v1 proves Kimi, Codex, WorkBuddy, and private platform Agent adoption paths are documented, privacy-safe, pack-indexed, and explicit that Mastra runtime, watcher daemon, and realtime HTML console are planned rather than shipped requirements

cognitive-loop-adoption-recipes-v1 gives platform Agents a machine-readable recipe matrix for first adoption, daily project review, risk decisions, and learning handoff without storing private context or real model keys

cognitive-loop-recipe-replay-verification-v1 proves the recipe matrix is safe for metadata-only replay, references existing scripts, resolves public acceptance evidence, and marks runtime or human-gated paths before execution

cognitive-loop-skill-entrypoint-verification-v1 proves the repo-local Skill, Codex pack, Kimi pack, WorkBuddy pack, ecosystem submission, bundle manifest, and adoption pack all expose the same Cognitive Loop recipe/replay entrypoint without storing private context or real model keys

cognitive-loop-recipe-cli-verification-v1 proves platform Agents can list and show Cognitive Loop recipe plans without executing recipe commands, starting runtime, applying file changes, storing private context, or taking real model keys
cognitive-loop-recipe-cli-receipts-v1 provides deterministic, redacted sample outputs and hashes for read-only recipe CLI import tests without executing recipe commands or taking real model keys
cognitive-loop-recipe-cli-failures-v1 provides deterministic, redacted negative-path receipts for recipe CLI import tests without executing recipe commands or taking real model keys

cognitive-loop-recipe-cli-schemas-v1 provides offline JSON Schemas for static platform-Agent validation of recipe CLI success, receipt, and failure reports without executing recipe commands or taking real model keys
agent-gateway-hardening-verification-v1 blocks unsafe provider config and bad Agent output

cognitive-loop-review-agent-workflow-install-smoke-v1 proves external adopters can extract the adoption pack, copy the manual Review Agent workflow into .github/workflows, and reproduce advisory/soft/strict metadata-only policy gate behavior without a repo checkout, raw report upload, real model call, or Study Anything model-key custody

launch-acceptance-ledger-v1 aggregates GitHub OSS, platform-Agent, self-host, support, release asset, and commercial boundary evidence without requiring a standalone frontend or Study Anything model-key custody

github-launch-operator-guide-v1 proves GitHub release operators have one local-first launch sequence, release asset list, platform-Agent distribution path, and no standalone frontend or Study Anything model-key custody
external-agent-adapter-hardening-v1 proves real external Agent eval evidence is separated from fake Agent evidence and bad outputs are diagnosed without leaking private data
notebooklm-obsidian-bridge-hardening-v1 blocks hidden instructions, dedupes fixture imports, and verifies strict handoff privacy

learning-enrichment-bridge-verification-v1 proves platform Agent enrichment inputs, Markdown/HTML micro-lessons, NotebookLM manual bridge, Obsidian/second-brain handoff, source hashes, and privacy evidence are aligned
plugin-quarantine-verification-v1 proves unknown plugins quarantine by default, explicit approval installs, and digest mismatch blocks copies
security-recovery-hardening-verification-v1 proves backup tamper detection, path traversal rejection, wrong passphrase redaction, and restore-preview privacy
platform-submission-dry-run-v1 proves Kimi, Codex, WorkBuddy-style, and generic OpenAPI packages are ready for manual submission without leaking private learning data
platform-manual-submission-rehearsal-v1 proves an operator can follow a redacted handoff checklist from unpacking through first lesson, external Agent setup, export evidence, and diagnostics

first-run-lesson-authoring-kit-v1 proves Kimi/Codex/WorkBuddy operators have copyable bilingual first-lesson prompts, tool-call sequence, context-package template, Agent setup, export evidence, and privacy assertions

external-eval-marketplace-harness-v1 proves native eval gates, optional Promptfoo/DeepEval/LangChain AgentEvals/Ragas adapters, fixtures, timeouts, redaction assertions, and marketplace-quality evidence are aligned
agent-eval-marketplace-enforcement-v1 proves optional and required external judge behavior, missing-runtime diagnostics, timeout policy, baseline regression, and privacy redaction are aligned

platform-adoption-feedback-diagnostics-v1 proves platform import failures, version drift, unsupported capability, local endpoint, missing command, and Agent eval evidence problems are diagnosable without private data
platform-feedback-package-v1 proves local-only feedback packages contain diagnostic summaries and redacted logs without source text, answers, prompts, personal profiles, or secrets
platform-import-failure-fixture-v1 proves common platform import failures are actionable and exclude source text, learner answers, agent prompts, endpoints, browser/video context, and model secrets
platform-field-adoption-rehearsal-v1 proves Kimi, Codex, WorkBuddy, and generic OpenAPI field rehearsals, import quirks, mock failed-import fixtures, and redacted support handoff are aligned
platform-support-triage-v1 proves GitHub-first support issue templates, support bundle contracts, maintainer response playbooks, and redacted support ticket fixtures are aligned
platform-support-ticket-fixture-v1 proves mock support tickets contain version, platform, command, diagnostic code, fixture id, redacted logs, and next commands without private learning data

platform-support-issue-template-v1 proves support templates ask for actionable diagnostics while forbidding raw source text, learner answers, Agent endpoints, model keys, and private browser/video context
platform-onboarding-readiness-v1 proves first external adopter walkthroughs, maintainer SLA labels, rotation checklists, release-blocker fixtures, and pack inclusion are aligned
platform-triage-dashboard-v1 proves support bundle completeness, diagnostic distribution, fixture coverage, privacy scan, and release blocker evidence are generated
platform-release-blocker-fixture-v1 proves release blockers are linked to support categories and can be reproduced without private learning data

plugin-ecosystem-adoption-kit-v1 proves bundled sample plugins, registry digests, quarantine-first trust policy, platform pack commands, and privacy evidence are aligned without executing plugin entrypoints

deployment-hardening-verification-v1 proves Skill Mode, published-image, source-build, Docker/Compose diagnostics, non-ASCII path recovery, port checks, GHCR manifest fallback, and local Agent endpoint guidance are aligned
pmf-readiness-v1 keeps hosted paid services not_ready

public-support-status-v1 proves public platform status, known blocker fixtures, SLA labels, commands, fixture hashes, and release readiness can be published without private support bundle or learning data
public-maintainer-dashboard-v1 proves a generated JSON/Markdown maintainer dashboard is metadata-only and privacy-safe
public-status-linkage-fixture-v1 proves SLA labels map to public status without exposing private support bundle fields
published-image-evidence-v1 proves manifest platforms, docker-images workflow, local pull timeout fallback, optional remote smoke replay, and release-blocking classifications are metadata-only

published-image-evidence-fixture-v1 proves manifest pass with local pull timeout, missing platform, docker-images failure, GHCR unavailable, remote smoke pass, and remote smoke failure states map to public release evidence
release-asset-adoption-v1 proves GitHub Release zip assets, sha256 digests, adoption-pack manifest hashes, published-image evidence, and replay commands are metadata-only

release-asset-adoption-fixture-v1 proves asset-only pass, missing asset, digest mismatch, corrupted pack, missing published-image evidence, and network-unavailable states map to public release evidence
release-asset-adoption-proof-v1 proves release asset replay can emit a redacted machine-readable result for metadata-only, published-image, and Skill Mode paths
release-asset-bootstrap-v1 proves external platform Agents can start from GitHub Release assets, verify import manifests, choose runtime mode, and get recovery commands without a development checkout
release-asset-bootstrap-transcript-v1 proves the bootstrap command emits a redacted machine-readable adoption transcript for Kimi, Codex, WorkBuddy, or generic HTTP tool hosts

platform-agent-release-replay-v1 proves external platform Agents can import release asset tools and replay the minimum learning tool chain against a running Study Anything API without leaking source text, answers, Agent secrets, model keys, or local paths

adopter-evidence-archive-v1 proves release proof, CI commands, Docker manifest evidence, platform pack checksums, public status hashes, known limitations, and maintainer handoff are packaged without private learning data
adopter-evidence-fixture-v1 proves successful release, local GHCR pull timeout, needs-repro, release-blocker, platform-blocked, and resolved support states map to public archive evidence

release-cleanroom-bootstrap-v1 proves external operators can start from GitHub Release assets without a prepared checkout, validate platform imports, run selected runtime paths, and emit a redacted issue-ready report
release-cleanroom-bootstrap-evidence-v1 proves the standalone bootloader, example report, classification matrix, recovery steps, and privacy boundary are packaged for external platform adoption
cognitive_loop_mastra_adapter.schema_version == cognitive-loop-mastra-adapter-verification-v1

cognitive-loop-mastra-runtime-dry-run-verification-v1 proves the metadata-only Mastra runtime rehearsal covers high-risk suspension, approved resume, rejected bail, and Event Store projection without starting Mastra or storing model keys

cognitive-loop-mastra-runtime-service-verification-v1 proves a repo-local Mastra runtime MVP can start through @mastra/core and cover high-risk suspension, approved resume, rejected bail, and low-risk no-gate paths without storing model keys

cognitive-loop-langfuse-observability-verification-v1 proves repo-local Mastra service and durable receipts map to Langfuse trace/span/generation/score DTOs without calling Langfuse or leaking source text, diffs, learner answers, Agent endpoints, Agent metadata, prompts, keys, storage paths, or absolute local paths

cognitive-loop-artifact-console-verification-v1 proves external operators can open a static metadata-only HTML Artifact Console Lite from Event Store, watcher runner, Study Adapter, DecisionCard, Human Gate, and LoopRun evidence without embedding private artifact contents, starting a daemon, requiring a standalone frontend, or taking model-key custody

commercial_readiness.monetization_boundary: Do not sell a standalone app during this phase. Commercial work should prepare future hosted sync, hosted publishing, team workspaces, and a trusted plugin ecosystem without locking the OSS workflow.

name: Study Anything Ecosystem Submission Pack

positioning.primary_user_experience: The external platform Agent remains the user's conversation, browsing, file, and tool surface. Study Anything runs as a local learning engine behind platform tools or a Codex Skill.
study_anything_owns: agent task contracts and output validation

known_limits: Browser-only Kimi chat cannot reach localhost directly.
Kimi or Moonshot credentials stay in the user's gateway or platform environment.
The submission pack does not include raw learning content or private platform context.
local_verification_commands: .venv/bin/python scripts/cognitive_loop_watcher_runner.py run --html --study-adapter --changed-path apps/api/study_anything/core/workflow.py --git-diff-summary "Metadata-only workflow boundary changed"
verification_commands: .venv/bin/python scripts/cognitive_loop_watcher_runner.py run --html --study-adapter --changed-path apps/api/study_anything/core/workflow.py --git-diff-summary "Metadata-only workflow boundary changed"

known_limits: Requires a terminal-capable Agent with local checkout access.
The Skill launches or calls a local Study Anything API instead of becoming a standalone chat product.

known_limits: The workspace must be able to call a local or private HTTP endpoint.
Admin, provider setup, sync export, restore preview, and PMF export endpoints are intentionally excluded.

known_limits: OpenAPI import behavior differs by host platform; use the tool catalog as the fallback mapping.
The pack is for local-first OSS adoption, not managed hosting or marketplace payments.

summary: Submission-ready local-first integration metadata for Kimi-compatible, Codex Skill, WorkBuddy-style HTTP, and generic OpenAPI tool platforms.
